judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable evidence and rigor; Report 2 adds a bit more nuance (weak reminders comparison, privacy angle on 'production data') but neither is clearly superior on the graded expectations.

report_1.evidence: **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" is false.** `app/routers/visits.py:1-11` shows `list_visits()` returning an empty list (`return []`) with a trailing comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo. ... **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" is false and contradicted by the code's own comment.** `app/security/config.py:1-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` followed by `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`.

report_2.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** — false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. ... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** — not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py` only has a `GET /visits` endpoint returning an empty list, with a trailing `# TODO: pdf export` comment. There is no export route, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo.

report_1.evidence: `before_scheduling.png`: {"route": "/schedule", ...} `after_scheduling.png`: {"route": "/billing/invoices", ...} I opened both images to confirm: `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely different screen (billing, not scheduling).

report_2.evidence: Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule` (the "before" shot's route). This is confirmed visually: `before_scheduling.png` literally renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES" — an entirely different page.

report_1.evidence: `after_export.png` is referenced in the document (line 26) but **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` (confirmed via `ls`). ... `unused_capture_07.png` exists in the img directory (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is **not referenced anywhere** in the document

report_2.evidence: `after_export.png` — referenced in the "Export your visit history" section, but this file does **not** exist in `docs/release/img/` ... `unused_capture_07.png` (renders "PATIENT LIST") exists in the img folder but is not referenced anywhere in the draft

report_1.evidence: **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (footer, line 33) is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content ... It should be stripped before send

report_2.evidence: the trailing line `Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance.` at the bottom of the doc (`highlights-v4.md:33`) is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing copy. ... It should be stripped before sending
